title: dolichos trilobus subsp. occidentalis
intro: Dolichos trilobus subsp. occidentalis, also known as the three-leafed bean or three-leafed dolichos, is a plant species native to Australia, characterized by its trifoliate leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions, wounds, and inflammation, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Dolichos trilobus subsp. occidentalis include its ability to soothe skin irritations, promote wound healing, and reduce swelling. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
